column1: TIER 2  |  WILD ADVANCED PRACTITIONER  |  $100  |  10 Modules  |  ~3 Hours Total
Target: Tier 1 graduates or people with prior WILD experience. Focuses on consistency, optimization, hardware, and cognitive performance application.

column6: Student can execute 3 WILD variants and understands which to use for each sleep window.
column9: WILD variants: SSILD, DEILD (Dream Exit Induced), WILD-WBTB combined. Shortened induction (under 5 min with REM pressure). Entry through audio, tactile, and visual channels. Troubleshooting failed transitions.
column12: Attempt DEILD this week. Log the difference in transition speed vs standard WILD. Document your fastest entry time.
column14: Complete Tier 1 or demonstrate hypnagogic awareness

column6: Student can sustain lucidity beyond 30 minutes using advanced stabilization stacking and intentional sleep cycling.
column9: Stacking stabilization techniques (spin + touch + declare). Re-entering a dream after brief wake. Chaining dreams. Recognizing early destabilization signals. Creating stable "base environments" to return to.
column12: Set intention to extend your next lucid to at least 15 minutes. Use all 3 stabilization techniques sequentially. Log duration.

column6: Student has 4 specific protocols for using lucid time for professional cognitive tasks: problem-solving, creativity, rehearsal, and emotional processing.
column9: Mental rehearsal protocol (sports + professional skills). Creative problem-solving session design. Architectural/design walkthroughs. Emotional processing protocol. How to set intention with enough specificity for REM to generate relevant content.
column12: Choose one professional task. Write a 3-sentence intention. Execute on next lucid. Journal insights on waking. Compare to waking attempts at the same problem.

column6: Student can manipulate their sleep schedule to maximize WILD-accessible REM cycles and track REM quality with existing wearables.
column9: REM cycle timing by sleep position (early vs late night). How sleep debt affects WILD access. Polyphasic approaches for practitioners. Using Oura/Whoop/Apple Watch data to optimize WBTB timing. Interpreting HRV data for WILD readiness.
column12: For 7 nights, record your wearable data alongside WILD attempts. Identify your personal optimal WBTB window. Share in Discord #sleep-data.

column6: Student understands the consumer neurotechnology landscape and how to integrate hardware tools as practice accelerators (not replacements for skill).
column9: Overview: Dreem 3, Neurosity Crown, Zmax Headband, Aurora Dreamband. How they work (REM detection + stimulus delivery). Honest cost-benefit analysis. DIY approaches: audio cues, light masks, phone apps. How hardware amplifies trained skill.
column12: Research one device that fits your budget. If owning one: log 7 nights with hardware-assisted vs unassisted WILD. Compare results.

column6: Student understands the evidence base for lucid dreaming supplements and how to safely use them as adjuncts to the protocol.
column9: Galantamine: mechanism, dosing (4-8mg at WBTB), evidence. Alpha-GPC: synergy with galantamine. Mugwort: traditional use + mild evidence. Vitamin B6: dream vividness effect. 5-HTP: REM rebound caution. What to avoid. Cycling approach to prevent tolerance.
column12: If comfortable: trial galantamine (4mg) on one WBTB night. Log effect on transition speed and lucidity. Compare tracked data to baseline.

column6: Student has a daily metacognitive practice that increases baseline self-awareness, making lucid entry faster and more reliable.
column9: Reality checks: which work (nose pinch, hand check, text check) and why. Metacognitive journaling as daytime WILD prep. Waking life awareness training. Critical question habit: "Am I dreaming?" How daytime practice maps to dream state.
column12: Install 3 reality check reminders daily for 2 weeks. Log whether REM success rate increases. Post data in Discord #technique-lab.

column6: Student can reliably navigate and modify the dream environment, summon specific people/objects, and use teleportation techniques.
column9: Expectation-based dream generation (the most reliable control mechanism). Teleportation: spinning, doors, mirrors. Summoning people or objects. Environmental stabilization. Working with dream characters as information sources. Limits of control.
column12: In your next lucid dream: intentionally change one element of the environment. Walk through a door expecting a new scene. Log whether it worked and what appeared.

column6: Student has a quantified self system for WILD that tracks attempt success rate, technique performance, and cognitive output quality.
column9: Advanced tracking template (beyond the 7-night log). Multi-cycle success rate analysis. Tracking cognitive output quality (ideas generated, problems solved). Integration with Notion or Obsidian. Feeding tracked data back to Claude API for pattern analysis.
column12: Set up the advanced tracking template in Notion or Excel. Run one full 30-night tracking cycle. Share your Month 1 success rate data in Discord.

column6: Student has a fully personalized, documented WILD system that they can maintain indefinitely and potentially teach to others.
column9: Personalizing the protocol based on your tracked data. Building a WILD habit stack. Your optimal pre-sleep stack (timing + techniques + supplements if used). Scaling frequency and depth of practice. Preview of Tier 3 and retreat preparation.
column12: Write your personal WILD system in one page: your optimal setup, best technique, WBTB timing, and success rate. Share in Discord #wins.
